perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement with a 30-day prior written notice to the other only after the first six months of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the earlier statement by adding conditions for termination. Initially, either party could terminate at any time with written notice. The perturbation now requires a 30-day notice period and restricts termination within the first six months of the agreement. This introduces ambiguity regarding the termination rights of both parties, conflicting with the original 'at any time' provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue until December 31, 2020, unless extended or earlier terminated. Either party may terminate this Agreement with a 30-day prior written notice to the other only after the first six months of this agreement. This Agreement may be extended upon mutual written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a restriction on termination that directly contradicts the original provision in the 'Contradicted Text'. While both texts are in the same location, the modified termination clause now has a waiting period and requires a 30-day notice after six months. This conflicts with the original agreement, which had the clause 'This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue until December 31, 2020, unless extended or earlier terminated'. Because there are now conditions to when the contract can be terminated, whereas before any party could terminate the agreement with a 30-day prior written notice, a strong contradiction exists.
